trade.summary: Bilateral trade has grown steadily, with India importing coal, palm oil, and minerals from Indonesia. India exports pharmaceuticals, machinery, and iron/steel products. Trade ties strengthened under ASEAN-India FTA.

diplomatic.summary: Both nations are large democracies and founding members of the Non-Aligned Movement. Cooperation spans ASEAN-India dialogue, G20 co-membership, and shared interests in maritime security and counter-terrorism. Relations are warm but not deeply institutionalized.

military.summary: Defence cooperation includes occasional joint naval exercises in the Indian Ocean, information sharing on maritime domain awareness, and defence industry dialogue. Both countries seek to secure key sea lanes.

financial.summary: Indian FDI in Indonesia has grown in the energy, IT, and pharmaceutical sectors. Indonesian sovereign wealth fund Danantara has explored co-investment opportunities with Indian counterparts.

energy.summary: Indonesia is a major coal supplier to India's power sector. Growing discussions on renewable energy cooperation including solar and biofuels.

scientific.summary: Modest academic and research collaboration, primarily through bilateral science agreements and joint university programmes. Space agency cooperation under discussion.
